steps.title: Match the table
steps.content: The table with $y=-3$ for $x=-6$, $x=-5$, and $x=4$ is choice B.

steps.title: Why these x-values are chosen
steps.content: The tables use $x=-6$, $-5$, and $4$ because those are exactly the zeros of $f$. At each one, the product contains a zero factor, so $f(x)-3=-3$.
